items.title: Archbishop Gustavo Oscar Carrara: ‘Argentina has 6,000 villas housing five million people’
items.description: Gustavo Oscar Carrara, the Metropolitan Archbishop of La Plata, warns about the defunding of urbanisation works in low-income neighbourhoods and the potential elimination of government department dedicated to socio-urban integration. Leer más

items.title: Trump's arch plan and the history of gateways
items.description: The proposed 250‑foot arch has sparked debate in Washington. But monumental gateways have shaped politics, memory, and ritual across cultures for millennia.

items.title: UN chief condemns killing of UNICEF worker in DR Congo strike
items.description: The Secretary-General expressed outrage over the death of UNICEF’s Karine Buisset, who was killed in a drone strike on a building housing aid workers in Goma, in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

items.title: Germany, others partially release oil reserves amid Iran war
items.description: Germany, Austria, Japan, Spain and France have all indicated plans to release some of their oil reserves, a day after the International Energy Agency was debating the pros and cons. The Iran war has driven prices up.

items.title: Spain withdraws ambassador to Israel as rift intensifies
items.description: Spain's government has formally dismissed its ambassador to Israel, six months after recalling her in a dispute prompted by the war in Gaza. Its Tel Aviv embassy will be led by a charge d'affaires until further notice.

items.title: Iran: Funeral procession held in Tehran for military officials
items.description: Thousands gathered in the Iranian capital for a funeral procession in memory of several top military leaders, the country's state media reported on Wednesday.

items.title: How Iran war laid bare the world's reliance on Gulf oil and gas
items.description: Countries around the world are feeling the impact of the conflict and the resulting energy price shock.

items.title: Ukraine opens battlefield data access to allies' AI models
items.description: Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov said ⁠a platform had been created to safely train AI models without giving away sensitive data

items.title: Germany to adjust heating law to allow fossil fuels
items.description: Fossil fuel prices are rising in response to global conflicts and military actions. Still, Germany is revising its policy for heating private homes to focus less on renewables. Climate activists are up in arms.

items.title: Israeli billionaire's firm settles DR Congo graft probe for $30 million
items.description: A former holding company of Israeli billionaire Dan Gertler is paying $30 billion to settle a Dutch corruption investigation over mining deals in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

items.title: Germany news: Lufthansa pilots stage another strike
items.description: A two-day strike by Lufthansa pilots is expected to lead to many cancellations. The German chancellor is visiting Norway, a key energy supplier to his country.

items.title: Swiss police probe if deadly bus fire was 'deliberate act'
items.description: A bus fire in the Swiss town of Kerzers killed at least six people and injured several others. Investigators say early indications point to a possible deliberate act.

items.title: Forensic team identifies 12 new dictatorship victims at Córdoba’s ‘La Perla’ site
items.description: Court in Córdoba confirms skeletal remains found at La Perla detention centre last year have been identified and pertain to 12 missing dictatorship victims; Former detention centre operated in the La Calera area, on the outskirts of the provincial capital. Leer más

items.title: Man arrested after vehicle crashes into White House gates
items.description: A man was taken into custody on Wednesday after driving his van into a security barrier outside the White House, authorities said.

items.title: ASEAN Ministers to hold meetings to address West Asia crisis
items.description: The Philippines, this year’s ASEAN chair, is ⁠hosting the meetings as officials weigh impacts and responses to surging oil prices as well as disruptions to shipping, logistics, and trade flows across the ‌region’s export-reliant economies

items.title: Rapper-politician Balendra Shah's party wins Nepal election
items.description: The Rastriya Swatatantra Party has won a large majority in an election dominated by issues including corruption.

items.title: Suspect killed after attack on Detroit-area synagogue
items.description: A rifle‑wielding man rammed his car into one of the largest Reform synagogues in the United States. The suspect was dead and a security guard was injured in the ramming.

items.title: Burundi's ex-Prime Minister  provisionally freed on medical grounds
items.description: Burundi's former Prime Minister General Alain-Guillaume Bunyoni, serving a life sentence for conspiracy against the head of state, was provisionally released Wednesday for medical reasons, judicial sources and relatives confirmed.

items.title: Suspect dead after ramming vehicle into Michigan synagogue
items.description: The FBI says the incident is being investigated as a "targeted act of violence against the Jewish community".

items.title: M23 rebel group accuses DRC government of targeting civilians after drone strike
items.description: In Goma, eastern DR Congo, a drone strike around 4 a.m. on Wednesday caused panic among residents.

items.title: Pressure grows on Trump as Iran warns of long war that would 'destroy' world economy
items.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard vows to target "economic centres and banks" that it deems linked to US and Israeli interests; US President Donald Trump said the conflict would end "soon," as Tehran effectively closes off Strait of Hormuz. Leer más

items.title: Oil stays above $100 a barrel amid Iran’s stranglehold on Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Energy markets remain on tenterhooks as the prospect of prolonged war in the Middle East grows.

items.title: Russia steps up drone attacks on Ukraine's railways
items.description: Russia has been targeting Ukraine's railways in an effort to disrupt the country's economy and supply lines. How is Russia conducting the attacks? And can the strikes be stopped?

items.title: News24 | Israel settlers use cover of Iran war restrictions to attack West Bank Palestinians
items.description: Israeli settlers in the occupied West Bank are taking advantage of curbs on movement imposed during the war on Iran to attack Palestinians, with military roadblocks preventing ambulances from reaching victims quickly, rights groups and medics say.

items.title: Milei government slams 'scam' gender policies in International Women’s Day message
items.description: Milei’s government hails December 2023 decision to abolish the Women, Gender & Diversity Ministry on the grounds that it promoted “absurd ideological agendas.” Leer más

items.title: News24 | Senegal lawmakers back tougher anti-LGBTQ+ law
items.description: Parliament approved legislation doubling prison sentences and increasing fines for same-sex relations. Activists say the move reflects a broader push for stricter anti-LGBT laws in the region.

items.title: Merz says Germany won't join EU return to nuclear energy
items.description: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen is in favor of a proposal to build new nuclear power plants in the EU. German Chancellor Friedrich Merz says it's impossible.

items.title: News24 | Israel strike kills 7 in Lebanon, Hezbollah announces major new operation
items.description: Lebanon said an Israeli strike on central Beirut’s seafront killed at least seven people, another attack in the heart of the capital as Iran-backed Hezbollah launched more missiles at Israel.

items.title: Chile’s new President José Antonio Kast to signal swift alignment with Trump
items.description: Kast’s new administration plans to sign broad agreements with the United States to boost cooperation on critical minerals and security matters. Leer más

items.title: UK MPs 'deeply troubled' by BBC World Service funding uncertainty
items.description: The current funding agreement between the BBC and the government expires at the end of this month.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Hozier, Jessie Buckley and Bruce Springsteen record Shane MacGowan tribute album
items.description: The album will also feature Primal Scream, David Gray, Jessie Buckley, Johnny Depp and Kate Moss.

items.title: Honda warns of $16bn hit on its pivot away from EVs
items.description: Japan's Honda expects to book extra costs of almost $16 billion as it recalibrates its electric vehicle strategy. It blamed US policy changes and tariffs in part, as well as reduced competitiveness in Asia.

items.title: Analysis:Why Japan's bar for yen intervention is now higher
US military announces rescue effort after fuelling aircraft crashes in Iraq
items.description: The Islamic Resistance in Iraq claims responsibility for shooting down the plane, no deaths reported so far.

items.title: Commentary: China’s AI lobster craze comes with claws
items.description: Fervour for AI agent OpenClaw has consumed China in recent weeks but underneath the excitement, there are concerns, says Catherine Thorbecke for Bloomberg Opinion.

items.title: Large shinbone found in New Mexico belongs to close kin of T. rex
Despite Trump's frustration, Spain's Pedro Sánchez doubles down on opposing the war
items.description: President Trump has been voicing his frustration with the Spanish government's opposition to the war in Iran. But far from backing down, Spain's prime minister is happy to walk into the spotlight.

items.title: DV attacker photographed victim in court, jailed for more than four years
items.description: A man who was found guilty of domestic violence offences and who pleaded guilty to contempt of court for photographing the victim giving evidence has been jailed for more than four years.

items.title: Is Argentina at war?
items.description: At a Jewish university in New York, President Milei once again described Iran as an “enemy” and declared that “we are going to win” the war in the Middle East. The lessons of the past and the risks of reckless ideological displays. Leer más

items.title: Netanyahu says new Iran leader 'can't show his face in public'
items.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says that Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei "can't show his face in public" after Tehran issued a first statement in his name that was read out by a TV presenter. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

items.title: News24 | ‘I don’t want to die in here’: Weinstein disputes rape conviction, calls prison life ‘hell’
items.description: Disgraced movie mogul and convicted rapist Harvey Weinstein says life in prison is “hell” in an interview where he repeatedly claimed he was not guilty of any crimes.

items.title: U.S. investigates strike on Iranian school as the war sparks a global oil crisis
items.description: Trump, who promised to lower gas prices, is tapping the Strategic Petroleum Reserve as war drives prices up. And, the U.S. investigates the strike on an Iranian school that killed at least 165 people.

items.title: Trump says Iran welcome at World Cup despite ongoing war
items.description: US President Donald Trump has said Iran is welcome to take part in the upcoming FIFA World Cup, despite the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: Trump urges Latin America to use military against cartels
items.description: Trump tells a group of Latin American leaders at security summit in Miami that Washington "will not tolerate the lawlessness in our hemisphere any longer." Leer más

items.title: Prosecution of trio linked to Dezi Freeman investigation stalls
items.description: Charges against people questioned amid a broader investigation into the fatal shooting of two police officers will not proceed due to a lack of evidence, Victoria Police has announced.
